title: Provider API key preview should reveal first & last chars
prompt:
In Settings → Providers, the API key field is fully masked so the user cannot tell which key is configured — they want to see the beginning and ending characters of the key (e.g. `sk-ab…wxyz`), like cc-switch / common secret-preview UIs.

Context from prior work (see memory 'provider-edit-dialog-cross-agent-collision-fixed', commit c8e39): the edit dialog now initializes from the per-agent config and `redactAgentProviderSets` emits a masked `apiKeyPreview` (the masked value is never persisted). The current preview appears to fully obscure the key.

Goal: change the masking so the preview shows a partial reveal — first N and last N characters with the middle replaced by a mask (e.g. `sk-pr…・・・…aB12`), choosing N safely for short keys (don't reveal too much of a short key). 

Where to look:
- `redactAgentProviderSets` / the `apiKeyPreview` masking logic (backend redaction).
- The Settings → Providers edit dialog field that renders `apiKeyPreview`.
- Ensure the raw key is still NEVER sent to the client or persisted — only the partial-reveal preview string.

Acceptance:
- Existing/saved provider shows a masked preview with visible head + tail and a masked middle.
- Very short keys are masked conservatively (no near-full reveal).
- Editing/saving still works and never round-trips a masked value back as the real key.
- web:typecheck + biome clean.
